English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Guido Kisch papers
Kisch, Guido, 1889-1985.
6314
2.4 cubic feet. 2.4 cubic feet.
[ca. 1934-1972]. Correspondence, manuscripts, publications. Correspondence, manuscripts, professional papers and writings, reviews, publishing correspondence, syllabi, essays, dissertations, notes, meeting agendas, and other papers. Collection material in English 3272126
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2015
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2015
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Born Prague, the son of Alexander Kisch, the Chief Rabbi of that city; Author; humanist scholar; Professor of law, Jewish history and bibliography, and Jewry law at the University of Leipzig, the University of Halle, and the Jewish Institute of Religion (New York), later Hebrew-Union College; also Honorary Professor at the University of Basel; founder and editor of Historia Judaica.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, manuscripts, professional papers and writings, reviews, publishing correspondence, syllabi, essays, dissertations, notes, meeting agendas, and other papers of Guido Kisch, relating to German legal history, humanist scholarship, Jewry law, publishing, and to his professional career as author and professor.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also: Guido Kisch Collection at the Leo Baeck Institute, Center for Jewish History, New York: http://digifindingaids.cjh.org/?pID=229838
Cite As:
Guido Kisch Papers, #6314. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Jewry-law -- Study and teaching. Jewish learning and scholarship. Jews -- History -- Germany. Jews -- Legal status, laws, etc. -- Germany. Law -- Study and teaching -- Germany. Jewish law -- Study and teaching Judaism -- History.
